entities.name: Capital Investment vs Idealism Frame
entities.context: Voice primitive expressing that incumbents won't sacrifice sunk capital/inertia for idealistic or logical alternatives

entities.name: Systemic Resistance to Change
entities.context: The broader phenomenon this primitive is used to explain — why entrenched players resist disruption

entities.name: Incumbents
entities.context: Established players with significant capital investment who resist change

entities.name: Capital/Inertia
entities.context: Sunk costs and organizational momentum that lock incumbents into existing paths

entities.name: Idealism/Logic
entities.context: The rational or principled case for change that incumbents nonetheless reject

entities.name: LinkedIn
entities.context: Source platform where this voice primitive was captured

entities.name: Entrepreneurship
entities.context: Domain where this frame applies — understanding why startups face incumbent resistance

entities.name: Voice Primitives
entities.context: V's system for cataloging distinctive rhetorical patterns and reusable conceptual frames

relationships.context: This frame is V's go-to lens for explaining why systems resist change despite better alternatives
Applied in entrepreneurship and startup domains to explain incumbent behavior
Originally sourced from V's LinkedIn content
Cataloged as a high-distinctiveness (0.95) conceptual_frame primitive
Incumbents are bound to their capital investment and organizational inertia
Incumbents reject idealism/logic when it threatens their capital position
The core tension in the frame — sunk cost vs rational alternative
